info.name: Starter
info.description:
API collection for the Inertia Starter project.

## Authentication
- All API requests require `X-API-TOKEN` header
- All requests require `Accept-Language` header (`en` or `ar`)
- Protected routes require Bearer token from login
- Login uses `identifier` field — searched across all columns configured in `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` env (comma-separated, e.g. `email,phone`)
- Registration requires each identifier field by name (e.g. `email`, `phone`)
- Additional profile fields (email, phone, username) can be toggled via `HAS_EMAIL_FIELD`, `HAS_PHONE_FIELD`, `HAS_USERNAME_FIELD` env variables (only for non-identifier fields)

## Variables
- `{{base_url}}` — Base URL (e.g. http://localhost:8000)
- `{{x-api-token}}` — API token for server authentication
- `{{token}}` — Bearer token from login response

item.name: Pages

request.description:
Returns all active pages with their translated content.

The `name` and `content` fields are returned in the language specified by the `Accept-Language` header.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Returns a single active page by its slug.

The `name` and `content` fields are returned in the language specified by the `Accept-Language` header.

Returns 404 if the page doesn't exist or is not active.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
Not Found

item.description: Endpoints for retrieving page content. Pages contain translatable content (name and HTML content) that adapts to the Accept-Language header.

request.description:
Returns all active languages available in the system. Use this to know which locales are supported for translations and the `Accept-Language` header.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Languages retrieved successfully.",
  "errors": null,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "code": "en",
      "name": "English",
      "native_name": "English",
      "direction": "ltr",
      "is_default": true,
      "image": null
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "code": "ar",
      "name": "Arabic",
      "native_name": "العربية",
      "direction": "rtl",
      "is_default": false,
      "image": null
    }
  ]
}

item.name: Translations

request.description:
Returns all translation key-value pairs for the current locale.

The locale is determined by the `Accept-Language` header. Returns a flat object of `key: value` pairs for the requested language.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success (English)
Success (Arabic)

request.description:
Create or update translations. Translations are stored in the `app` group.

**Request format:** JSON object where each key is the translation key, and the value is an object with either:

1. **`default`** — applies the same value to ALL active languages:
```json
{
  "hello": { "default": "Hello" },
  "welcome": { "default": "Welcome!" }
}
```

2. **Specific language codes** — only updates those languages:
```json
{
  "goodbye": { "en": "Goodbye", "ar": "مع السلامة" },
  "thanks": { "en": "Thanks" }
}
```

**Rules:**
- Cannot mix `default` with specific language codes in the same key
- Each key must have either `default` OR language codes (not both, not empty)
- Only active languages in the system will be updated
- Existing keys are updated, new keys are created

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
Success with Errors
Empty Body

item.description: Endpoints for managing mobile app translations. Translations are stored in the `app` group and returned based on the `Accept-Language` header.

item.name: Auth
Verified

request.description:
Request an email change. Sends an OTP to the new email address for verification.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Verify the email change OTP and update the user's email.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Update the authenticated user's profile info.

**Available fields:** `name`, plus any non-identifier fields that are enabled (e.g. if identifier is `username`, you can update `email` and `phone` if those fields are enabled).

The identifier field itself cannot be changed through this endpoint.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Change the authenticated user's password.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Permanently delete the authenticated user's account and revoke all tokens.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Get all social accounts linked to the authenticated user.

Returns an array of social accounts with provider info and whether more can be linked based on configuration.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success
No Social Accounts

request.description:
Link a social account (Google, Apple, etc.) to the authenticated user's account.

**Required:** `token` — Firebase ID token from the social provider.

**Constraints:**
- Social account email must match the user's account email
- Social account must not already be linked to another user
- Provider must be in the allowed providers list (configurable via DevSettings)
- User cannot exceed max social accounts limit (if set)
- Same provider cannot be linked twice to the same user
- Requires email to be configured as a login identifier

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success
Email Mismatch
Provider Not Allowed
Provider Already Linked
Max Accounts Reached
Already Linked to Another User

request.description:
Unlink a specific social account from the authenticated user.

**Required:** `provider` — The provider to unlink (e.g. `google.com`, `apple.com`)

**Constraints:**
- User must have a password set OR have other social accounts linked (cannot remove last login method)
- The specified provider must be linked to the user

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success
Provider Not Linked
Cannot Unlink (No Password)

item.description: Endpoints that require the user's email to be verified.

request.description:
Get the currently authenticated user's profile.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Operation successful",
  "errors": null,
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Test User",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "phone": null,
    "verified_at": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000000Z",
    "is_active": true,
    "created_at": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000000Z"
  }
}

request.description:
Send an OTP to the authenticated user's email for account verification.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent to the user's email to complete account verification.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Logout the authenticated user and revoke the current access token.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Register a new app user.

**Required fields:** `policy_agreed`, `name`, `password`, `password_confirmation`, plus each configured identifier field (e.g. `email`, `phone`).

**Optional:** `fcm_token`, and any non-identifier fields that are enabled (e.g. `username`).

Identifier fields are configured via `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` env variable (comma-separated, e.g. `email,phone`). All identifier fields are required and unique.

Returns a Bearer token on success. An OTP is sent for verification (email/phone identifiers only).

response.name: Success

request.description:
Login an existing app user.

**Required fields:** `identifier`, `password`

**Optional:** `fcm_token`, `remember_me`

The `identifier` field is searched across all configured identifier columns (set via `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` env, e.g. `email,phone`). Users can log in with any of their configured identifiers.

Returns a Bearer token on success.

response.name: Success
Invalid Credentials

request.description:
Login or register using Firebase social authentication (Google, Apple, etc.).

**Required:** `token` — Firebase ID token from the social provider.

**Optional:** `fcm_token`

**Prerequisites:**
- Email must be configured as a login identifier (`AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` must include `email`)
- Firebase project must be configured with credentials in `config/firebase.php`

**Behavior:**
- If social account is already linked → login
- If email exists with password → returns error (must use password login)
- If email exists without password (social-only) → links and logs in
- If email doesn't exist → creates new account

Returns a Bearer token on success. User is automatically verified.

response.name: Success (New User)
Success (Existing User)
Provider Not Allowed
Account Exists (Use Password)
Invalid Token
Email Required
Social Auth Not Available

request.description:
Check if an identifier exists in the system. Searches across all configured identifier columns (set via `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` env).

Useful for real-time validation on the login screen before submitting credentials.

No authentication required.

response.name: Exists
Not Found

request.description:
Request a password reset OTP. Sends an OTP to the user's email or phone based on `IS_EMAIL_LOGIN` env.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent for password reset. Must be called before `Change Forgot Password`.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Set a new password after verifying the forgot password OTP.

**Required:** `email`, `otp` (verified), `password`, `password_confirmation`

No authentication required.

response.name: Success

item.description: User authentication endpoints including registration, login, password reset, OTP verification, and account management.
